lane_0_pressure.reason: NO_PROXY failed; evaluate keytar/OAuth route; keytar only relevant after token-mode reset to user_access_token

lane_2_current_lark_config.config_state_detail: Lark MCP in stdio mode with --token-mode tenant_access_token; NO_PROXY present (R147G patch); OAuth not enabled

token_mode_reset_options.name: Remove --token-mode, keep LARK_TOKEN_MODE env=tenant_access_token
token_mode_reset_options.config_change: Remove --token-mode tenant_access_token from CLI args; keep env
token_mode_reset_options.expected_effect: SDK behavior unchanged; still uses tenant_access_token; 99991661 persists

token_mode_reset_options.name: Switch to --token-mode user_access_token
token_mode_reset_options.expected_effect: OAuth flow activated; requires browser interaction and keytar working

token_mode_reset_options.name: Set token_mode to auto (remove explicit mode)
token_mode_reset_options.expected_effect: SDK in auto mode; may attempt UAT (user access token) first; keytar issue means user token unavailable; fallback to tenant; 99991661 persists

token_mode_reset_options.name: Keep tenant mode, abandon keytar route
token_mode_reset_options.expected_effect: 99991661 persists; Lark MCP remains non-functional in current config

lane_3_token_mode_reset_analysis.critical_insight: In tenant_access_token mode, keytar is completely irrelevant. The keytar rebuild route only helps if we also switch to user_access_token mode AND complete OAuth login. These are coupled requirements, not independent.

lane_4_keytar_feasibility.keytar_fix_conclusion: keytar.node binary is missing from pnpm cache. Rebuild may succeed but requires native compilation tools (node-gyp, Python, C++ compiler) which may not be present. Even if rebuild succeeds, it only enables OAuth token persistence — it does not fix the tenant token auto-attach bug.

lane_5_oauth_feasibility.oauth_flow_description: Starts local HTTP server on localhost:3000 → opens browser for OAuth → callback to localhost:3000/callback with auth code → token stored via keytar
lane_5_oauth_feasibility.keytar_prerequisite: keytar.node must exist for OAuth token storage to work after login completes

lane_6_repair_route_comparison.name: keytar rebuild + switch to user_access_token mode + OAuth login
lane_6_repair_route_comparison.reason: Requires native rebuild (may fail without build tools), requires user browser interaction, still has 99991661 risk if SDK tenant token bug exists in user mode too. Too many coupled failure points.

lane_6_repair_route_comparison.name: Explicit USER_ACCESS_TOKEN env injection
lane_6_repair_route_comparison.reason: Would work but requires injecting a real user access token into env — security risk and token would be visible in config

lane_6_repair_route_comparison.name: Investigate SDK withTenantToken() pattern and patch mcp-tool.js
lane_6_repair_route_comparison.reason: Would require deep SDK investigation and code modification; potentially most correct fix but heaviest lift

lane_6_repair_route_comparison.name: Pause Lark, investigate Tavily 405
lane_6_repair_route_comparison.reason: Lark MCP tenant token bug is a deep SDK issue requiring multi-step repair with uncertain outcome. Tavily 405 was identified early (R131A) and is independent. Pausing Lark unblocks progress on other fronts.

lane_6_repair_route_comparison.name: Pause external MCP, return mainline
lane_6_repair_route_comparison.reason: External MCP debug is consuming significant phase budget without resolution. Return to mainline to make forward progress, can revisit later.

lane_7_recommended_strategy.recommended_strategy: Pause Lark MCP (disable it in extensions_config.json); enable Tavily MCP; investigate Tavily 405 error from R131A
lane_7_recommended_strategy.rationale: Lark MCP 99991661 has persisted across R147D→R147G despite multiple fix attempts (NO_PROXY, token mode). The root cause is a deep SDK behavior issue: getShouldUseUAT=false causes handler.js to call func(params) without token, and there's no evidence Client.request() auto-attaches tenant token for stdio MCP tools. The keytar rebuild route is blocked because: (1) keytar.node binary missing from cache, (2) rebuild may fail without build tools, (3) even if keytar works, OAuth login requires browser interaction, and (4) token mode must also be switched to user_access_token which is a separate config change with its own risks. Tavily 405 was identified in R131A and is independent — worth investigating while Lark is paused.
lane_7_recommended_strategy.next_action: R147I: disable Lark in extensions_config.json, enable Tavily, smoke test Tavily

validation_plan: 3. Load tools and verify tavily_search is available
5. If 405: Tavily 405 confirmed → investigate R131A notes
6. If success: Tavily working → Lark paused successfully
7. If other error: classify and determine next step

lane_9_branch_decision.rationale: Lark MCP is blocked by deep SDK token attachment bug. Multiple fix attempts (NO_PROXY, token mode changes) have failed. Pausing Lark and switching to Tavily unblocks MCP tool availability. Lark can be revisited when more resources/time are available for a proper SDK patch investigation.

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: keytar module is only used for OAuth user token persistence, NOT for tenant_access_token flow. keytar.node missing does NOT cause 99991661 in tenant mode.
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147H: keytar relevance correctly classified — keytar rebuild does not fix tenant token issue

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: Switching to user_access_token mode requires --oauth flag AND OAuth login AND keytar working — these are coupled requirements
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147H: identified that keytar rebuild alone is insufficient; must also switch token mode AND complete OAuth login

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: keytar.node binary missing from pnpm cache; rebuild requires native build tools; success uncertain
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147H: keytar rebuild feasibility assessed as medium risk with uncertain outcome

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: lark-mcp OAuth login requires browser redirect to localhost:3000/callback — requires human interaction and cannot be automated without additional tooling
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147H: OAuth login requires user action (browser) which is blocked by current phase constraints

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: SDK's handler.js calls func(params) without token when getShouldUseUAT=false; Client.request() may not auto-attach tenant token; this is the root cause of 99991661
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147H: confirmed root cause is SDK not auto-attaching token, NOT proxy or keytar

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: Lark MCP tenant token issue is deep SDK bug; Tavily 405 (R131A) is independent and should be investigated while Lark is paused
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147H: recommended pause Lark + investigate Tavily
